title: Unidentified stormtrooper commander (Tantive IV)
content:

{{Quote|The Death Star plans are ''not'' in the main computer.|The stormtrooper commander, to Darth Vader|<ref name="ANH" />|audio=NotInMainComputer.flac}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] '''stormtrooper commander''' served as an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps's]] elite [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He served on the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']] under the command of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], during a [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|battle]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|Commander]] led a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Imperial troops]] to besiege the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], the [[Consular ship/Legends|consular ship]] of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Once the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] had been secured, the [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] discovered that the stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], for which the Empire was searching, were not in the ship's main [[Computer/Legends|computer]] and reported this to Vader. Vader ordered the Commander to continue searching for the plans and to bring him the ship's passengers alive.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Commander, tear this ship apart until you've found those plans, and bring me the passengers, I want them alive!|Darth Vader, to the Commander|<ref name="ANH" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CVD" /> [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|commander]] served as an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="ANH" /> He<ref name="ANH novel" /> was assigned to the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], an elite [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Legion/Legends|unit]] unofficially known as "Vader's Fist," under the direct command of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] enforcer.<ref name="BF2" /> The [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] was part of the [[Troop/Legends|troop]] complement of<ref name="ANH" /> the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], Vader's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]].<ref name="BTM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref> At some point by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tatooine]] to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> he acquired a facial scar from an [[Laser/Legends|energy beam]] that penetrated the shielding of his [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|helmet]].<ref name="ANH novel" />

[[File:NotIntheMainComputer.png|thumb|left|250px|The Commander reports to Vader that the stolen plans are not in the main computer.]]
In 0 BBY,<ref name="NEC" /> after [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents transmitted the stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] to the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], [[Consular ship/Legends|consular ship]] of the Rebel-affiliated [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], the ''Devastator'' pursued and [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|captured]] the Rebel [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="ANH" /> This stormtrooper helped lead the 501st Legion as the assault commander<ref name="DS">[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']]</ref> during the boarding and securing of the Rebel starship.<ref name="BF2" /> He reported the ongoing attempt to [[Slicer/Legends|breach]] the ''Tantive IV''{{'s}} entry locks to Vader and escorted him to the main [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] to board the captured vessel.<ref name="DS" />

Stormtroopers forcibly boarded the ''Tantive'' and captured Rebel [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]].<ref name="ANH" /> Vader then came aboard and the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] proceeded to the corridor where the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] were being held.<ref name="DS" /> The assault commander meanwhile led a team to the ship's control room to check its main [[Computer/Legends|computer]] for the Death Star plans.<ref name="ANH novel" />

As Vader held Captain Antilles by the throat to interrogate him, the assault leader rushed over from the control room and reported that the plans could not be found.<ref name="ANH novel" /> Antilles denied Vader's accusation that he had intercepted illegal transmissions before [[Death/Legends|dying]] by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] hand. The enraged Vader ordered the assault commander to search for the plans more thoroughly and to bring him the vessel's passengers alive. Although Organa was captured by stormtroopers just [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] later, the troopers were unaware that the stolen plans were already on their way to Tatooine in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] with Organa's [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]].<ref name="ANH" />

==Personality and traits==
The commander had a scar under his helmet where an energy weapon had penetrated his armor. He feared his commanding officer, Darth Vader. After killing Antilles, Vader whirled around, and the nearby Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officers]], including this stormtrooper commander, shrank under his stare. Both officers and troops scurried away to carry out the Dark Lord's orders—and to retreat from his malevolent presence.<ref name="ANH novel" />

==Equipment==
The commander wore [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]] and was armed with an [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]].<ref name="ANH" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The ship's information retrieval system has been wiped clean!|The commander, to Darth Vader, in the Marvel comic ''Star Wars 1''|<ref name="starwars1977-1">[[Star Wars (1977) 1|''Star Wars'' (1977) 1]]</ref>}}
[[File:StormtrooperCommanderManga-ANH.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Another commander ordered this stormtrooper commander to report to Vader—a scene that appeared only in the ''Manga'' comic.]]
The character of the stormtrooper commander first appeared in the [[December]] [[1976]] [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']],<ref name="ANH novel" /> ghostwritten by [[Alan Dean Foster]].<ref name="ANH novel SE">[[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' novelization]], [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|''Special Edition'']] foreword by [[George Lucas]]</ref> In the novel, the Commander is described as an officer whose "armored helmet [was] shoved back to reveal a recent scar, where an energy beam had penetrated his shielding," and he speaks a different line than his dialogue in the film.<ref name="ANH novel" />

The character's first live-action appearance occurred in the film ''A New Hope'' itself, in which he was portrayed by an uncredited extra.<ref name="ANH" /> His scene was one of the last few of principal photography, filmed sometime between [[June 29]] and [[July 16]], 1976 in the [[Wikipedia:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]].<ref name="making-definitive">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref> The character's line was performed by [[Wikipedia:Disc jockey|disc jockey]] [[Terence McGovern]],<ref name="terry">{{WebCite|url=http://www.terrymcgovern.com/starwars.html|text=Terry McGovern Home Page|work=[https://www.terrymcgovern.com/ Terry McGovern]|archivedate=20171217125955}}</ref> said over a walkie-talkie and recorded by sound designer [[Ben Burtt]].<ref name="sound">''[[The Sounds of Star Wars]]''</ref> In the revised fourth draft script of ''A New Hope'', as presented in ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'', the Commander is identified as "Imperial Officer."<ref name="BTM" />

In the [[Black Knight, White Princess, and Pawns|third episode]] of the [[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars'' radio drama]], the stormtrooper commander's line is given by an unmasked Imperial officer,<ref name="Black Knight">{{SWRadio|Black Knight, White Princess, and Pawns}}</ref> identified in the [[Star Wars: The National Public Radio Dramatization|script]] as "Commander,"<ref name="radio script">[[Star Wars: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''Star Wars'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]]</ref> as part of a longer scene where a commander with the same voice actor accepts Antilles' surrender before Vader arrives. The same actor also voices the Imperial commanders [[Daine Jir/Legends|Daine Jir]] and [[Nahdonnis Praji]] elsewhere in the episode,<ref name="Black Knight" /> and this article makes no assertion whether it is Jir, Praji, or this article's subject who accepts Antilles' surrender and also treats it as unclear if the article's subject is the character with the corresponding film line, given the unmasked voice.
